zh.lifeSummary: Richard Browne是Henry Browne Or Brown和Mrs Agnes Browne的兒子，於1547年出生在英國盧特藍德諾斯 盧費納姆。他於1572年6月22日與Agnes Mary Eliza Wing在英國盧特藍德諾斯 盧費納姆結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他埋葬在英國艾可艾爾姆斯亞德。

nameMeanings.name: Browne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably also an Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Braune   or   Braun  .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

variants.name: Brown
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: generally a nickname referring to the color of the hair or complexion, Middle English br(o)un, from Old English brūn or Old French brun. This word is occasionally found in Old French, Middle English and Old Norse as a personal name or byname (Middle English personal name Brun, Broun, ancient Germanic Bruno, Old English Brūn, or possibly Old Norse Brúnn or Brúni). Brun- was also an ancient Germanic name-forming element. Some instances of Old English Brūn as a personal name may therefore be short forms of compound names such as Brūngar, Brūnwine, etc. As a Scottish and Irish name, it sometimes represents a translation of Gaelic Donn (see below). Brown (including in the senses below) is the fourth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below).
Irish and Scottish: adopted for Ó Duinn (see Dunn ) or for any of the many Irish and Scottish Gaelic names containing the element donn ‘brown-haired’ (also meaning ‘chieftain’), for example Donahue .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: Richard Browne was born in 1547, in North Luffenham, Rutland, England as the son of Henry Browne Or Brown and Mrs Agnes Browne. He married Agnes Mary Eliza Wing on 22 June 1572, in North Luffenham, Rutland, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He was buried in Elmstead, Essex, England.

Il a épousé Agnes Mary Eliza Wing le 22 juin 1572, à North Luffenham, Rutland, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Ilenterré à Elmstead, Essex, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Browneは1547年にイギリスルトランドノース ルフエンハムでHenry Browne Or BrownとMrs Agnes Browneの息子として生まれた。1572年6月22日にイギリスルトランドノース ルフエンハムでAgnes Mary Eliza Wingと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼はイギリスエセックスエルムステアドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Browne(은)는 1547년 영국 루틀랜드 노르스 루펜함에서 Henry Browne Or Brown(와)과 Mrs Agnes Browne의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1572년 6월 22일 영국 루틀랜드 노르스 루펜함에서 Agnes Mary Eliza Wing(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 에세스 엘스테아드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Browne родился в 1547 г. в местности норт луффенам, рутланд, Англия. Его родителей звали Henry Browne Or Brown и Mrs Agnes Browne. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Mary Eliza Wing 22 июня 1572 г. в местности норт луффенам, рутланд, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он был похоронен в местности элмстид, эссекс, Англия.
